aliases: how the regin command line is wired
where do CLI command bodies live
blurb: Adding, grouping, or debugging a `regin` CLI command, or running doctor / init / rebuild
intent: Explain how the `regin` CLI is assembled and how to extend it: the thin `cli/regin.py` entrypoint that re-execs under the project `.venv` and fixes sys.path, the Typer root in `cli/app.py` that wires per-domain modules under `cli/commands/` (each exposing `register(app)` for flat commands or a pre-built sub-Typer for `regin <group> <cmd>`), the root callback that stamps every invocation into the activity log, and the shared `cli/deps.py` (`require_db` guard) + `cli/output.py` (testable echo/error/table sinks) helpers. `doctor`, `init --force`, and `rebuild --clean` are the canonical ops/lifecycle commands, backed by `lib/doctor.py` and `lib/db_rebuild.py`.
label: regin CLI architecture & command surface (Typer entrypoint, venv re-exec, per-domain command registry, doctor/db lifecycle, add-a-command playbook)
